concerns.text: T016 attempts to load 'data/results/dense_baseline.json' as a prerequisite for US3 metrics, but the pipeline lacks a task to GENERATE this artifact. T008b only fetches/validates the raw dense data source, and no task exists to run the dense baseline inference to produce the JSON results file. This creates a consumer-before-producer violation where T016 cannot execute.

T017 computes WorldScore for the dense baseline and Sparse-Consistency for the sparse method, but there is no preceding task that generates the 'sparse method' output artifacts (warped frames/3D points) required for T017 to calculate the Sparse-Consistency Score. T013/T014 (US2) produce intermediate geometry data, but no task aggregates this into the final 'sparse results' artifact that T017 consumes.

T020 (Orchestrator) is placed in Phase 6 (Integration) but depends on T016/T017 (US3) which are in Phase 5. However, T020 is described as consuming 'completed artifacts from phases T008-T019'. The ordering implies T020 runs AFTER T019, but T020 is also the task that 'aggregates results from both sparse and dense paths'. If T017 (which computes the metrics) runs before T020, T020 is redundant. If T020 runs before T017, it cannot aggregate metrics that don't exist yet. The dependency chain for the final aggregation is circular or missing a 'run dense inference' step.

T008b is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the availability of the 'dense depth baseline' data which is an external assumption. If the external data is missing, T008b fails, blocking T016. While not a strict internal dependency, the [P] tag implies it can run independently of the rest of the pipeline, but it actually has a hard external dependency that might not be met, making the parallel execution unsafe without a pre-check.
